mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS changes coincide with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals highly accommodative monetary policy that compresses risk-free discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts capture broad demand disintegration that reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery regimes, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal manufacturing re-acceleration that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession periods, month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE above 9% coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects aggregate demand expansion that translates into positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth surprises fail to precede positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and consumer prices rise simultaneously, negative real interest rates stimulate borrowing and risk-taking, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and rising CPIAUCSL coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power (t-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 1% signals imminent supply-side contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines exceeding 1% coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
